title: On responsible machine learning datasets emphasizing fairness, privacy and regulatory norms with examples in biometrics and healthcare
methods: fairness quantification (Shannon diversity index + inclusivity + label reliability)
data_sources: 60 computer vision datasets (52 face-based biometric + 8 chest X-ray healthcare)
FPR rubric code and CSV files (osf.io/pfujw)

data_scope.datasets_surveyed: over 100 computer vision datasets initially; 60 included after filtering

core_conclusion: A large-scale audit of 60 computer vision datasets reveals that virtually all biometric and healthcare datasets fail on fairness, privacy, and regulatory compliance: 91% score 2/5 or below on fairness and 89% score 0–1/3 on regulatory compliance. The study introduces a quantitative FPR responsible rubric, identifies a fundamental fairness–privacy paradox, and calls for wholesale revision of dataset creation methodologies.
why_it_matters: First joint quantitative audit unifying fairness, privacy, and regulatory compliance into a 3-tuple score; reveals systematic failures invisible to single-metric audits; exposes the structural fairness–privacy paradox; provides actionable open rubric and code at a critical global regulatory moment.
why_nature: Timely intersection of AI policy, law, and technical auditing; novel joint FPR framework validated at scale across 60 datasets; broad applicability across CV and healthcare AI; platform-type contribution with open rubric and code.
added_value_to_my_research: Extend FPR rubric with location-privacy and representational-equity axes to audit geospatial mobility datasets used in GeoAI and 2SFCA/R2SFCA accessibility studies
Formalize fairness-privacy paradox as Pareto optimization over dataset design choices — methodology parallels my EV-charging efficiency-equity optimization
Evaluate federated learning readiness of human-centric datasets to enable privacy-preserving fusion of distributed health and mobility data for telehealth accessibility modeling
Build a living FPR re-audit observatory to track responsible data practice over time, providing temporal evidence for FAIR data governance in my spatial datasets
Apply SHAP-based attribution to decompose FPR scores by annotation decision, creating interpretable design-time guidance — extending my explainable GeoAI pipeline to data curation

figures.caption: Overview of the responsible ML dataset audit framework — quantifying responsibility across fairness, privacy, and regulatory compliance dimensions
Quantification framework detail — factors for fairness (inclusivity, diversity, labels), privacy (six attribute types), and regulatory compliance

DBSCAN cluster analysis in 3D FPR space — 5 clusters with FB Fairness Dataset and UTKFace closest to the ideal reference point
